skill.name: paywall-upgrade-cro
skill.description: Guides creation and optimization of in-app paywalls, upgrade screens, upsell modals, trial expiration prompts, usage-limit gates, and feature-lock flows.
skill.summary: Optimize in-app paywalls and upgrade prompts after users experience product value.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backticks, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found false positives in a single Markdown guidance file. The backticks are fenced UI mockups, and the other hits are ordinary paywall, CRO, and UX discovery terms. No prompt injection, executable code, network calls, secret handling, or data exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Paywall Mockups Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backtick locations are Markdown fenced examples that show paywall screen copy and layout. They do not contain shell commands, Ruby execution, user input interpolation, or instructions to execute code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged regions are visible Markdown code fences with bracketed UI placeholders and plain upgrade-copy examples. No executable syntax or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Paywall CRO Text Misidentified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain skill metadata, upgrade benefits, design sections, and trust-related paywall guidance. No weak cryptographic algorithms, hash functions, encryption APIs, or credential handling appear at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line contexts are product-growth and design guidance, not code. The static labels appear caused by keyword collisions rather than cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: UX Discovery Guidance Misidentified as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged system and network reconnaissance lines are business discovery questions, mobile UX guidance, and paywall trigger descriptions. They do not instruct the model to inspect a host system, scan networks, enumerate services, or collect sensitive environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context shows ordinary conversion optimization and product research prompts. No commands, network targets, endpoint probing, or system enumeration instructions were found.

content.user_title: Optimize In-App Paywalls and Upgrade Flows
content.value_statement: Free users often see upgrade prompts before they understand the value. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design respectful paywalls that connect timing, copy, UX, and testing.
actual_capabilities: Maps paywall recommendations to feature gates, usage limits, trials, team seats, and soft prompts.
Suggests upgrade screen components such as headlines, value previews, plan choices, CTAs, and exit paths.
Provides mobile paywall guidance for full-screen flows, tap targets, subscription terms, and restore purchases.
Defines timing, frequency, and dismissal rules to reduce user frustration.
Proposes A/B test ideas for trigger timing, layout, pricing display, copy, trial structure, and personalization.
Outlines output sections for paywall design, upgrade flow, copy variants, and experiment plans.
limitations: It does not implement checkout, billing, analytics, or subscription infrastructure.
It cannot verify app store compliance without current platform policy review.
It needs product context, pricing, user journey data, and conversion metrics for precise recommendations.
It provides CRO guidance, not legal, tax, or payment-processing advice.

use_cases.title: Improve a Freemium Feature Gate
use_cases.description: Create a clearer paid-feature prompt with value preview, plan context, CTA copy, and a respectful free-plan exit.

use_cases.title: Refine Trial Expiration Messaging
use_cases.description: Design reminder and expiration screens that summarize value received, explain what changes, and offer a direct upgrade path.

use_cases.title: Plan Paywall Experiments
use_cases.description: Generate testable hypotheses for timing, layout, pricing presentation, copy, dismissal behavior, and personalization.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Upgrade Prompt
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a single paid feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an in-app upgrade prompt for a free user who clicks [paid feature]. Include headline, body copy, CTA, value preview, and dismiss option.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Existing Paywall
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for improving a current screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this paywall copy and flow: [paste details]. Identify timing, clarity, trust, pricing, CTA, and exit-path issues. Suggest a revised version.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Trial Expiration Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for a multi-step upgrade sequence.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a trial expiration flow for [product]. Include 7-day, 3-day, 1-day, expired, and reactivation states with copy and UX notes.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Experiment Roadmap
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for CRO planning across segments.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a paywall experiment roadmap for [product]. Use these metrics: [metrics]. Include hypotheses, variants, target segments, primary metrics, and guardrails.

output_examples.input: A user reaches the free project limit in a SaaS workspace app.
output: Recommended prompt: show the current limit, explain the Pro benefit, and preserve a delete-project path.
Primary CTA: Upgrade to Pro. Secondary action: Manage projects.
Test idea: compare unlimited-project framing against collaboration-benefit framing.

output_examples.input: A mobile app trial ends in three days.
output: Recommended flow: reminder banner, value summary screen, final-day prompt, and expired-state reactivation offer.
Trust notes: show subscription terms, restore purchases, cancellation language, and clear pricing.
Copy direction: emphasize completed actions and continued access, not fear or guilt.

best_practices: Show upgrade prompts after users have experienced clear product value.
Make pricing, trial terms, cancellation, and downgrade paths easy to understand.
Measure conversion, churn, annoyance signals, and post-upgrade activation together.
anti_patterns: Do not hide close buttons or make free-plan continuation hard to find.
Do not use misleading urgency, guilt-based copy, or surprise charges.
Do not show repeated prompts immediately after a user dismisses one.

faq.question: What types of paywalls does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers feature locks, usage limits, trial expiration, soft prompts, team upgrades, and mobile paywalls.

faq.question: Can it write complete paywall copy?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft headlines, body copy, CTA text, objection handling, and exit-path copy.

faq.question: Does it replace analytics or experimentation tools?
faq.answer: No. It helps plan what to test, but it does not collect data or run experiments.

faq.question: Is this only for SaaS products?
faq.answer: No. It can support SaaS, mobile apps, subscriptions, usage-based products, and freemium tools.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the trigger, pricing model, free-plan limits, paid features, conversion metrics, and current screen copy.

faq.question: Can it help avoid dark patterns?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance emphasizes clear exits, honest pricing, respectful timing, and transparent subscription terms.
